message:
fix(bv): scope err variable correctly and normalize dir in RunBd

- Move `var err` declaration above the dir-empty check in run() so
  cmd.Run() can reuse it (fixes compilation error with :=)
- Use normalizeTriageDir() in RunBd() for consistent cache keys
- Fix test expectation: single-dash `-robot-triage` matches actual flag
- Add restart_pane_test.go for bead assignment integration tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3no20","title":"LimitDetector: Pattern matching with configurable regex","description":"## LimitDetector: Pattern Matching with Configurable Regex

### Purpose
Implement pattern matching logic that detects usage limits in captured pane output.

### Implementation Details

**File:** Part of `internal/swarm/limit_detector.go`

**Pattern Matching:**
```go
// PatternMatcher checks output against limit patterns.
type PatternMatcher struct {
    // patterns maps agent type to compiled regexes
    patterns map[string][]*regexp.Regexp
}

// NewPatternMatcher creates matcher from config.
func NewPatternMatcher(limitPatterns map[string][]string) *PatternMatcher {
    pm := &PatternMatcher{
        patterns: make(map[string][]*regexp.Regexp),
    }
    
    for agentType, patternStrs := range limitPatterns {
        for _, p := range patternStrs {
            // Use case-insensitive matching
            re := regexp.MustCompile("(?i)" + regexp.QuoteMeta(p))
            pm.patterns[agentType] = append(pm.patterns[agentType], re)
        }
    }
    
    return pm
}

// Match checks if output matches any pattern for the agent type.
func (pm *PatternMatcher) Match(agentType, output string) (matched bool, pattern string) {
    patterns := pm.patterns[agentType]
    for _, re := range patterns {
        if re.MatchString(output) {
            return true, re.String()
        }
    }
    return false, ""
}
```

**Default Patterns:**
```go
var DefaultLimitPatterns = map[string][]string{
    "cc": {
        "You've hit your limit",
        "You have hit your limit",
        "usage limit",
        "Rate limit exceeded",
        "Please wait",
        "This conversation is getting long", // Context limit
    },
    "cod": {
        "You've hit your usage limit",
        "You have reached your usage limit",
        "Rate limit exceeded",
        "context limit reached",
    },
    "gmi": {
        "quota exceeded",
        "rate limit",
        "context window exceeded",
    },
}
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log pattern match: `[LimitDetector] pattern_matched agent_type=%s pattern=%q`
- Log no match: `[LimitDetector] no_match agent_type=%s` (debug level)

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Case-insensitive pattern matching
- [ ] Configurable patterns via config file
- [ ] Return matched pattern for logging
- [ ] Support both string literal and regex patterns","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:00:59.034358872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:35:41.641621235Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:35:41.641559559Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3no20","depends_on_id":"bd-11y0m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3no20","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3nson","title":"Unit Tests: ntm assign command strategy selection and bead-agent matching","description":"## Test Coverage
Test the core ntm assign algorithm for strategy selection and bead-to-agent matching.

## Test Cases

### Strategy Selection
- Select balanced strategy by default
- Select speed strategy when --strategy=speed
- Select quality strategy when --strategy=quality
- Select dependency strategy when --strategy=dependency
- Select round-robin strategy when --strategy=round-robin
- Error on invalid strategy name

### Bead-to-Agent Matching
- Match beads to idle agents only
- Respect agent type filters (--cc-only, --cod-only, --gmi-only)
- Apply capability matrix for quality strategy
- Prioritize high unblock_count for dependency strategy
- Even distribution for round-robin strategy

### Edge Cases
- Zero beads available
- Zero idle agents
- More beads than agents
- More agents than beads
- Single agent, multiple beads
- Multiple agents, single bead

### Output Format
- JSON output includes assigned array
- JSON output includes skipped array with reasons
- Each assignment has pane, bead_id, agent_type

## Logging Requirements
- Log strategy selected
- Log agent pool before matching
- Log each matching decision with score/reason
- Log final assignment map

## Test File

-{"id":"bd-3o9b0","title":"Implement bead assignment integration","description":"Implement bead assignment integration with --bead flag.

## Feature
When --bead=<ID> is provided, automatically send bead assignment prompt after restart.

## Prompt Template
Read AGENTS.md, register with Agent Mail. Work on: <BEAD_ID> - <DESC>.
Use br show <BEAD_ID> for details. Use ultrathink.

## Implementation
1. Fetch bead info via br show --json
2. Format prompt with bead ID and description
3. Send prompt to pane after agent is ready
4. Return bead_assigned and prompt_sent in response

## Alternative: --prompt flag
Allow custom prompt instead of bead template for flexibility.

## Error Handling
- If bead ID doesn't exist: return error before restart
- If prompt send fails: note in response but still report restart success","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:25.108162363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:15:10.576361352Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3o9b0","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3oeov","title":"EPIC: REST API v1 (Full CLI/Robot Parity)","description":"## Summary\nExpose the full NTM feature surface via a REST API that mirrors robot mode responses and supports idempotency, jobs, and OpenAPI documentation.\n\n## Scope\n- API server scaffolding with chi router, middleware, error envelope, request IDs.\n- Core resources: system, sessions, panes, agents, output tooling, palette/history, checkpoints, metrics, context, git.\n- Tool bridges: Agent Mail + reservations, Beads/BV, CASS/CM, Scanner, Accounts, Safety, Pipelines.\n- OpenAPI 3.1 spec generation + Swagger UI at /docs.\n- Consistent envelopes + error codes aligned with robot mode.\n\n## Success Criteria\n- Every CLI/robot feature has a REST endpoint.\n- OpenAPI spec is generated from kernel and stays in sync.\n- Idempotent POSTs support Idempotency-Key.\n- Long‑running ops return job handles.\n","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:48:20.934770050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T23:18:27.607825603Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-12vyy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-15amw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-1lxvl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-204ic","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-2q9v1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-2vzaq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-30zp4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-38p9u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-3cw6i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-3fqg9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-3hsvc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-3iomn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-6z2bf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-f8ikz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ol6","title":"E2E Tests: Full task assignment workflow","description":"E2E Tests for Full task assignment workflow.

## Test Scenarios

1. Basic assignment flow
   - Create test beads with bv
   - Spawn agents
   - Run ntm assign
   - Verify tasks distributed
   - Verify file reservations created

2. Priority-based assignment
   - Create P1, P2, P3 tasks
   - Run assignment
   - Verify P1 tasks assigned first
   - Verify P3 tasks queued

3. Dependency respect
   - Create tasks with dependencies
   - Run assignment
   - Verify blockers assigned before blocked

4. Interactive mode
   - Run ntm assign --interactive
   - Simulate user selections
   - Verify chosen assignments applied

## Detailed Logging
```
[E2E-ASSIGN] Loading tasks from bv: found N
[E2E-ASSIGN] Assigning TASK_ID to AGENT (priority=N)
[E2E-ASSIGN] Reserved files for AGENT: FILES
[E2E-ASSIGN] Assignment complete: N tasks to N agents
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 4 test scenarios implemented
2. Basic flow creates file reservations
3. P1 tasks assigned before P2/P3
4. Dependencies respected (blockers first)
5. Interactive mode responds to simulated input
6. Detailed logging with [E2E-ASSIGN] prefix
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. E2E tests verify full task assignment
2. BV-driven assignment tested
3. Assignment persists correctly
4. Assignment notifications sent
5. Test cleanup removes assignments
6. Test duration under 5 minutes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:15:21.439072364Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T03:34:36.568982216Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T03:34:36.568475522Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ol6","depends_on_id":"bd-27ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ol6","depends_on_id":"bd-9tbv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3pa8p","title":"Task: Unit tests for robot verbosity/short-keys/pagination","description":"## Overview
Add unit tests for robot output ergonomics features: verbosity profiles, short‑key mappings, and pagination/limits.

## Tests
- `--robot-verbosity=full|terse` toggles optional fields as specified
- Short‑key map produces stable, reversible keys (if enabled)
- Pagination returns deterministic `next_cursor` + correct `count`/`total` behavior
- Large payloads respect limits without breaking envelope

## Logging
Use `t.Logf` with structured prefixes:
- `t.Logf("ROBOT_TEST: verbosity=%s size=%d", mode, size)`
- `t.Logf("ROBOT_TEST: shortkeys=%t fields=%v", enabled, keys)`
- `t.Logf("ROBOT_TEST: page limit=%d next=%s", limit, next)`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic fixtures for each verbosity level
- Pagination behavior covered for edge cases (empty, exact limit, overflow)
- Tests run without external dependencies","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:24:13.235689238Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:24:42.797092773Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pa8p","depends_on_id":"bd-20ong","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pa8p","depends_on_id":"bd-2dovu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pa8p","depends_on_id":"bd-2tb5i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pa8p","depends_on_id":"bd-4uecx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pa8p","depends_on_id":"bd-9l7cp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3sl6a","title":"Task: Write E2E test for observability metrics","description":"E2E tests for coverage map, redundancy score, velocity metrics, and conflict density.

## Test Harness
- Use deterministic fixtures (no real model calls)
- Feed synthetic ModeOutput + token counts through metrics pipeline
- Run via existing e2e suite in `e2e/`

## Test Cases

### Coverage Map
```go
// e2e/ensemble_metrics_test.go
func TestE2E_Metrics_CoverageMap(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: fixed mode assignments with known categories
    // Verify: Coverage map shows correct category coverage
    // Verify: Blind spots correctly identified
}
```

### Redundancy Score
```go
func TestE2E_Metrics_RedundancyScore_LowRedundancy(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: outputs with disjoint findings
    // Verify: Low redundancy score
}

func TestE2E_Metrics_RedundancyScore_HighRedundancy(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: overlapping findings + evidence pointers
    // Verify: High redundancy score + suggestions
}
```

### Findings Velocity
```go
func TestE2E_Metrics_FindingsVelocity(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: outputs with known findings + token counts
    // Verify: Velocity per mode + overall
    // Verify: Underperformers flagged
}
```

### Conflict Density
```go
func TestE2E_Metrics_ConflictDensity(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: conflict list from AuditReport (preferred) + fallback heuristic
    // Verify: Conflicts detected and counted
    // Verify: High-conflict pairs identified
}
```

### Post-Run Report
```go
func TestE2E_Metrics_PostRunReport(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: complete ensemble run with metrics summary
    // Verify: Report includes all metrics + suggestions
}
```

## Logging
- t.Logf metric inputs (mode IDs, tokens, findings counts)
- t.Logf computed metric values
- Log threshold comparisons and selected suggestions

 {"id":"bd-3t4w3","title":"caut: Add dashboard widget for quota/spend visualization","description":"Add dashboard widget showing quota usage and cost across providers.

## Design

### Widget Location
Lower section of dashboard, expandable panel.

### Visual Layout
```
┌─ Usage & Costs ──────────────────────────────────┐
│ Provider    Usage      Tokens        Cost (USD)  │
│ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────│
│ Claude     [███░░] 45%  45K/100K     $12.50      │
│ OpenAI     [████░] 80%  160K/200K    $8.00   ⚠️  │
│ Gemini     [█░░░░] 10%  10K/100K     $2.00      │
│ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────│
│ Today: $22.50    Month: $150.00   (Est: $225)   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Features
- Progress bars for usage percentage
- Color coding: green/yellow/red
- Warning icon for >80% usage
- Monthly cost projection
- Clickable rows for detailed breakdown

## Implementation

### Model
```go
type QuotaWidget struct {
    cache    *caut.UsageCache
    width    int
    height   int
    expanded bool
}
```

### View
Use lipgloss for table and progress bars.

## Code Location
- internal/tui/widgets/quota.go

 {"id":"bd-3u59m","title":"Implement AutoRespawner for limit-hit recovery","description":"## AutoRespawner: Core Interface for Agent Recovery

### Purpose
Implement the AutoRespawner that handles automatic agent recovery when usage limits are hit. Integrates with LimitDetector events and AccountRotator for seamless recovery.

### IMPORTANT: Leverage Existing Code
NTM already has `internal/context/rotation.go` with:
- PaneSpawner interface for creating/killing panes
- DefaultPaneSpawner implementation
- SendKeys for sending commands to panes

AutoRespawner should USE these existing abstractions, not duplicate them.

### Implementation Details

**File:** `internal/swarm/auto_respawner.go`

**Interface:**
```go
// AutoRespawner handles automatic agent recovery.
type AutoRespawner struct {
    // Config holds respawn settings
    Config *config.SwarmConfig
    
    // LimitDetector provides limit events
    LimitDetector *LimitDetector
    
    // AccountRotator switches accounts (optional)
    AccountRotator *AccountRotator
    
    // PromptInjector re-sends marching orders
    PromptInjector *PromptInjector
    
    // PaneSpawner from context package (REUSE EXISTING)
    PaneSpawner context.PaneSpawner
    
    // eventChan emits respawn events
    eventChan chan RespawnEvent
}

// RespawnResult tracks a respawn attempt.
type RespawnResult struct {
    Success         bool          `json:"success"`
    SessionPane     string        `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType       string        `json:"agent_type"`
    AccountRotated  bool          `json:"account_rotated"`
    PreviousAccount string        `json:"previous_account,omitempty"`
    NewAccount      string        `json:"new_account,omitempty"`
    Duration        time.Duration `json:"duration"`
    Error           string        `json:"error,omitempty"`
    RespawnedAt     time.Time     `json:"respawned_at"`
}
```

**Respawn Sequence:**
```
1. Receive LimitEvent from LimitDetector
2. Log respawn start
3. Kill stuck agent:
   a. Send /exit (for codex) or Ctrl+C twice (for claude)
   b. Wait 2s for graceful exit
   c. If still running, kill process via PID
4. (Optional) Rotate account via AccountRotator
5. Clear pane: tmux send-keys -t <pane> 'clear' Enter
6. Respawn agent: Send agent command (cc/cod/gmi)
7. Wait for agent ready (configurable delay, default 5s)
8. Re-inject marching orders via PromptInjector
9. Emit RespawnEvent
10. Log respawn complete
```

**Kill Sequences by Agent Type:**
```go
func (r *AutoRespawner) killAgent(sessionPane, agentType string) error {
    switch agentType {
    case "cc":
        // Claude: Double Ctrl+C with 100ms gap (CRITICAL timing)
        r.PaneSpawner.SendKeys(sessionPane, "\x03", false)
        time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
        r.PaneSpawner.SendKeys(sessionPane, "\x03", false)
    case "cod":
        // Codex: /exit command
        r.PaneSpawner.SendKeys(sessionPane, "/exit", true)
    case "gmi":
        // Gemini: Escape then Ctrl+C
        r.PaneSpawner.SendKeys(sessionPane, "\x1b", false)
        time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
        r.PaneSpawner.SendKeys(sessionPane, "\x03", false)
    }
    return nil
}
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log respawn start: `[AutoRespawner] respawn_start session_pane=%s agent_type=%s`
- Log kill attempt: `[AutoRespawner] killing_agent session_pane=%s method=%s`
- Log account rotation: `[AutoRespawner] account_rotated old=%s new=%s`
- Log agent spawn: `[AutoRespawner] spawning_agent session_pane=%s`
- Log prompt injection: `[AutoRespawner] injecting_prompt session_pane=%s`
- Log respawn complete: `[AutoRespawner] respawn_complete session_pane=%s success=%t duration=%v`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Uses existing PaneSpawner interface from context package
- [ ] Agent-specific kill sequences
- [ ] Configurable timeouts and delays
- [ ] Integration with AccountRotator (optional)
- [ ] Integration with PromptInjector
- [ ] Event emission for audit trail
- [ ] Max retry limits per pane
- [ ] Detailed structured logging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:01:13.205417120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:40:18.538092929Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T03:40:18.537904134Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: AutoRespawner with agent-specific kill sequences, configurable timeouts, AccountRotator integration, PromptInjector integration, event emission, max retry limits, and comprehensive logging. All tests passing.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3u59m","depends_on_id":"bd-150ij","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u59m","depends_on_id":"bd-1bruk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u59m","depends_on_id":"bd-1v922","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u59m","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3u5r","title":"E2E Tests: Agent Mail auto-registration on spawn","description":"## Test Coverage
E2E test of auto-registering agents with MCP Agent Mail on spawn.

## Test Scenario
1. Ensure Agent Mail server running
2. Spawn agents: --cc=2 --cod=1
3. Verify agents registered in Agent Mail
4. Verify correct agent names (adjective+noun)
5. Verify pane-to-name mapping stored

## Verification
```bash
# Check Agent Mail for registered agents
am agents list --project=/test/path

# Expected output shows 3 agents with names like:
# - GreenCastle (pane 2, claude)
# - BlueLake (pane 3, claude)
# - RedStone (pane 4, codex)
```

## Prerequisites
- Agent Mail MCP server running
- Test workspace created

## Logging
- Log registration API calls
- Log agent names assigned
- Log pane-to-name mapping
- Log any registration failures

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-3u6dg","title":"Implement overall health summary and aggregation","description":"Implement overall health summary and aggregation.

## Summary Fields

- total_panes: count of agent panes (excludes control pane 1)
- healthy: count of healthy panes
- rate_limited: count
- unresponsive: count
- crashed: count

## Overall Health Classification

| overall_health | Criteria |
|----------------|----------|
| healthy | All panes healthy |
| degraded | Some issues but >50% healthy |
| critical | <50% healthy OR any crashed |

## Panes Grouping

Group pane indices by status for easy iteration:
```json
{
  "panes": {
    "healthy": [2, 3, 5, 6],
    "rate_limited": [4],
    "unresponsive": [7],
    "crashed": []
  }
}
```

## --brief Flag

When --brief, only return summary without per-pane recommendations:
```json
{
  "overall_health": "degraded",
  "summary": {...},
  "panes": {...}
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:27:57.896820274Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.646066312Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.646006259Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-31e1f diagnose.go - includes --diagnose-pane flag, overall health classification, --diagnose-fix auto-remediation, recommendation generator, and health classification engine","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3u6dg","depends_on_id":"bd-31e1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
